title: Under centos, calls the user.Lookup function before initializing libpod Runtime, and an error will occurs when calling this function again
body:
### Issue Description

I am using Podman's Runtime to customize my own program, and I found a problem in the process. The example code:
```
fmt.Println("call method user.Lookup before initial libpod runtime.......")
u, e := user.Lookup("yinruixing.bd")
if e != nil {
	fmt.Println(e)
} else {
	fmt.Println(u)
}

fmt.Println("init libpod runtime engine.......")

cfg := registry.EngineConfig()

engine, err := engine.NewEngine(cfg)
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println("new engine error", err)
}
fmt.Println("call method user.Lookup after runtime engine.......")
u, e = user.Lookup("yinruixing.bd")
if e != nil {
	fmt.Println(e)
} else {
	fmt.Println(u)
}
```
And engine is the set of ImageEngine and ContainerEngine：
```
type RuntimeEngine interface {
  ImageEngine() entities.ImageEngine
  ContainerEngine() entities.ContainerEngine
}
```
Under CentOS, an error will occurs, the output here is: 
```

However, the test program runs success under Debian. The log is bellow.
```
call method user.Lookup before initial libpod runtime.......
&{1001 1001 yinruixing.bd  /home/yinruixing.bd}
init libpod runtime engine.......
call method user.Lookup after runtime engine.......
&{1001 1001 yinruixing.bd  /home/yinruixing.bd}
```
If you does not call user.Lookup function before init the libpod Runtime, the error will not occur. The code is:
```
fmt.Println("init libpod runtime engine.......")

cfg := registry.EngineConfig()

engine, err := engine.NewEngine(cfg)
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println("new engine error", err)
}
fmt.Println("call method user.Lookup after runtime engine.......")
u, e = user.Lookup("yinruixing.bd")
if e != nil {
	fmt.Println(e)
} else {
	fmt.Println(u)
}
```
It seems that the different behavior is caused by the `mygetpwnam_r` cgo function

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. under Centos system
2. call user.Lookup before init libpod Runtime
3. after init libpod Runtime, call user.Lookup again, then the `segmentation violation` error will occur during cgo excution.

the build tags:
```
CGO_ENABLED=1 go run \
-tags "seccomp selinux apparmor containers_image_openpgp exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper exclude_disk_quota" \

Sorry but we do not support using podman/libpod code directly, https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/doc.go.
Also centos 7 is no longer supported as well.

We only support the REST API and pkg/bindings as stable interface.
